title: Chroma embedded mode
decision: Use ChromaDB in embedded mode, persistent at .orchid/chroma/
rationale: No extra infrastructure needed, fits homelab zero-infra principle

title: Embedding model priority
decision: llama.cpp nomic-embed-text on port 8081, sentence-transformers fallback
rationale: Keeps embeddings local and free, fallback ensures graceful degradation

title: Auto embed and recall
decision: Auto-embed session log on save, auto-recall on session load
rationale: Makes memory passive — agents benefit without explicit recall calls

title: Agent delegation pattern and depth limiting
decision: Agents emit 'Action: delegate[agent_type | task]' in the ReAct loop. AgentDelegator spawns the sub-agent, runs it with max_sub_iterations=5, logs and embeds the result, then returns it as an observation. Depth tracked per-agent and passed to sub-agents; capped at delegation.max_depth (default 3).
rationale: Enables cross-agent knowledge sharing without blocking the parent agent. Depth cap prevents infinite delegation loops.

title: Sub-context slimming strategy
decision: AgentDelegator._build_sub_context() passes: task description, depth indicator, top-3 vector recall hits for the task, and 1000-char slice of parent context. Never passes full parent ReAct trace.
rationale: Sub-agents need focus, not noise. Full parent traces bloat the context and distract sub-agents from their narrow task. Vector recall provides relevant history more efficiently.

title: Process-per-project parallelism
decision: Use Python multiprocessing.Process to run each project in an isolated worker. Each worker has its own Session, Orchestrator, agents, and file handles. No mutable state is shared across processes.
rationale: Session, Orchestrator, and agents are stateful objects; file-based state is per-project and never shared. Process isolation is the only safe model. Thread-based parallelism would require locking all state, creating deadlock risk.

title: Claude API rate limiting via multiprocessing.Semaphore
decision: Workers acquire a shared multiprocessing.Semaphore before every Claude API call. The semaphore is monkey-patched into orchid.tools.models.call inside each worker process. Local llama.cpp calls bypass it (stateless HTTP, unlimited).
rationale: Claude API has rate limits. Running N projects in parallel would multiply API calls by N without throttling, causing 429 errors. Semaphore(max_concurrent_claude_calls=3) is a simple, effective cap. Monkey-patching avoids adding semaphore parameter to the entire call chain.

title: Notification routing via multiprocessing.Queue
decision: Workers push {event, project, data} dicts to a shared multiprocessing.Queue. The coordinator drains the queue in its main loop and calls notification_callback(notification). multi_formatter tags all messages with [project] prefix (10-char max).
rationale: Workers cannot directly call async Telegram send functions (different process/event loop). A queue decouples workers from the notification sink. The coordinator bridges the gap, enabling console output, Telegram, or any future sink without changing worker code.

title: Slack Socket Mode — no public URL required
decision: Use slack-bolt SocketModeHandler for the Slack interface instead of HTTP webhooks.
rationale: Socket Mode requires only an app-level token (xapp-) and maintains a persistent WebSocket connection. No public URL, ngrok, or reverse proxy needed. Fits homelab/bare-metal deployment where inbound ports are unavailable.

title: Thread-per-task Slack progress updates
decision: Each task started via Slack gets its own thread. Progress and completion replies go in that thread.
rationale: Posting every agent iteration to the channel creates noise. Thread-per-task keeps the channel clean: one summary line per task plus all detail in the thread. Matches how engineers naturally track work in Slack.

title: BackgroundRunner shared between Telegram and Slack
decision: Both Telegram bot and Slack bot use BackgroundRunner for all agent execution.
rationale: BackgroundRunner provides the thread-pool/asyncio bridge needed for non-blocking agent runs. Sharing the abstraction avoids duplicating lifecycle management, notification dispatch, and inject logic in each interface layer.

title: FastAPI + React + WebSocket stack for web UI
decision: Use FastAPI for REST API and WebSocket, React (Vite) for frontend, served together at port 7842.
rationale: FastAPI's async support handles WebSocket and REST in one process. React + Vite gives a modern SPA with fast builds. No separate backend/frontend processes in production — FastAPI serves the pre-built dist/.

title: Vite builds dist/ served directly by FastAPI StaticFiles
decision: React frontend is built with npm run build to web_ui/dist/. FastAPI mounts the dist/ directory as static files.
rationale: Eliminates need for a separate Node.js process in production. Single orchid web command starts everything. Dev mode uses Vite proxy to forward /api and /ws to FastAPI.

title: Traefik file provider for orchid.scheidy.com
decision: Use Traefik bare-metal file provider (scripts/traefik-orchid.yml) to route orchid.scheidy.com to localhost:7842 with TLS via cloudflare certResolver.
rationale: Dave's setup uses Traefik as bare-metal binary with file provider — no Docker labels. The file provider config hot-reloads without restarting Traefik.

title: Provider Registry with 5-layer resolution
decision: Implement ProviderRegistry singleton with 5-layer resolution chain: CLI flag → project config → env var → task-type default → agent-type hardcoded fallback. ProviderBase ABC with 60s availability cache.
rationale: Decouples agents from hardcoded model endpoints, enables per-project provider config, supports offline mode, and gives clear escalation path for future providers.

title: All provider backends share ProviderBase interface
decision: Anthropic, LocalLlama, Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, and Bedrock all implement ProviderBase. boto3 is a lazy import for Bedrock — not a hard dependency in pyproject.toml.
rationale: Avoids making boto3 a hard requirement for users who only use local/Anthropic providers. Lazy import pattern gives clear install instructions at runtime.

title: CLI provider flags: --check-providers, --offline, --provider
decision: Add --check-providers (probe all providers, print status table), --offline (force all agents to local), --provider agent=provider (per-agent override). /api/providers web endpoint.
rationale: Gives operators visibility into provider health, a safe fallback for local-only runs, and fine-grained routing without editing YAML.

title: Auto-discovery via watchdog
decision: ProjectDiscovery scans watch_dirs (depth 2) for .orchid.yaml, watches for created/deleted events via inotify (debounced 2s). Excludes .venv/node_modules/.git/__pycache__. Explicit projects bypass .orchid.yaml requirement.
rationale: Eliminates manual --project flags for persistent service; projects self-register by adding .orchid.yaml.

title: orchid serve unified entry point
decision: orchid serve combines web UI + auto-discovery + AgentManager in one process. systemd service at scripts/orchid-serve.service. watch_dirs configurable via CLI --watch-dir or serve.watch_dirs config.
rationale: Single command replaces manually running orchid web + specifying every project. Enables hands-off bare-metal deployment.

title: APScheduler for cron-based auto runs
decision: AgentManager uses APScheduler BackgroundScheduler for cron-based project auto runs. Per-project persistent config in .orchid.yaml persistent: section (enabled, auto_run, auto_run_schedule, auto_run_code_model). Each run executes in a daemon thread.
rationale: Standard cron syntax familiar to users; BackgroundScheduler runs in a thread so the web server stays single-process.
